_comment: Ratchet config for scripts/codecharta-ratchet.py, run against the CodeCharta map (ortus.cc.json.gz). Three gates: (1) per-file aggregate complexity, (2) per-FUNCTION complexity (not gameable by moving code between files), (3) hotspot (complex AND under-tested), plus go coverage. Ratchet by LOWERING the numbers as the code improves; RAISING a baseline entry needs review justification.

complexity._default_cap_note: Per-file SUM of function complexity. Every file not listed below must stay <= this. This gate stops a file becoming a monolith, but can be satisfied by splitting a file (the sum moves with the code) — see function_complexity for the non-gameable ceiling. Current highest non-baseline file is 40 (internal/adapters/storage/http.go).
complexity._raster_repository_note: raster/repository.go raised 143→149 for the persistent extraction-cache feature (Open now branches ephemeral/persistent). The bulk of the new logic is in cache.go; the per-function cap stayed 15 (no function grew) — genuine new branching, not relocation.

function_complexity._default_cap_note: The single most complex FUNCTION in a file (McCabe). This is the real overall-complexity control: a function keeps its complexity wherever it lives, so moving it between files can't game this gate — the only way to pass is to actually simplify the function (or extract cohesive sub-functions). New functions must stay <= 10; the baseline freezes today's offenders at their current max so they can't grow, and should ratchet DOWN as they are simplified. Repo max today is 15.

hotspot._note: A file is a hotspot when it is complex AND under-tested; new hotspots fail the build. Grandfathered files below are the current ones — remove them from 'allow' as their coverage improves.
